description: OneDrive for Business CLI via Microsoft Graph API

auth.usage_instructions: Authentication management for OneDrive. Use `auth login` to authenticate via device code flow before any other commands. Use `auth status` to verify you're authenticated. Use `auth logout` to clear cached tokens.

login.help: Configure authentication credentials. Prompts for required credentials based on the tool's authentication type. For OAuth authorization code flows, opens a browser for user consent.
login.usage_instructions: Use to authenticate before any other OneDrive operations. Opens a device code flow — the user will be given a code to enter at microsoft.com/devicelogin. Must be run interactively.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to save credentials to

logout.help: Clear stored credentials and browser sessions.
logout.usage_instructions: Use to clear cached authentication tokens when switching accounts or troubleshooting auth issues.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to clear credentials from

profiles.usage_instructions: Run `onedrive auth profiles --help` for exact arguments and options.

create.help: Create a new profile from .env.example template.
create.usage_instructions: Run `onedrive auth profiles create --help` for exact arguments and options.

delete.help: Delete a profile and its data.
delete.usage_instructions: Run `onedrive auth profiles delete --help` for exact arguments and options.

get.help: Get details for a specific profile.
get.usage_instructions: Run `onedrive auth profiles get --help` for exact arguments and options.

arguments.name: name
arguments.help: Profile name to get details for

list.help: List all profiles and show which is the default.
list.usage_instructions: Run `onedrive auth profiles list --help` for exact arguments and options.

options.name: --limit
options.help: Maximum number of profiles to return

options.name: --properties
options.help: Comma-separated list of fields to include

remove.help: Delete a profile and its data.

select.help: Activate a profile within its auth type.

arguments.name: name
arguments.help: Profile name to activate within its auth type

status.usage_instructions: Use to verify authentication is working before running other commands. Use --table/-t for formatted output. Returns account info if authenticated.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to check (defaults to all profiles)

test.help: Test authentication by verifying credentials work across profiles.
test.usage_instructions: Run `onedrive auth test --help` for exact arguments and options.

cache.usage_instructions: Run `onedrive cache --help` for exact arguments and options.

clear.help: Remove all cached responses. Deletes every file in the cache directory ({storage_dir}/cache/) and reports how many files and bytes were freed.
clear.usage_instructions: Run `onedrive cache clear --help` for exact arguments and options.

drives.usage_instructions: Drive management — list accessible drives and get drive details. Use `drives list` first to find your drive ID, which is required by most item and folder commands.

get.help: Get details for a specific drive.
get.usage_instructions: Use to get detailed info about a specific drive by its ID, including quota and owner info. DRIVE_ID is a required positional argument. Use --properties/-p to select specific fields.

list.help: List all drives accessible to the authenticated user. Shows personal OneDrive and any shared drives the user can access.
list.usage_instructions: Use to discover available drives and their IDs. The drive ID is required for most items and folders commands. Use --table/-t for formatted output. Default limit is 100. Use --properties/-p to select specific fields like id, name, driveType.

options.name: --limit
options.help: Maximum number of drives to return

folders.usage_instructions: Folder management — create folders, get folder metadata, create sharing links, and invite people to folders. Some commands use drive ID as positional arg, others use --drive/-d option. Use `folders get` to check if a folder exists (exits with code 1 if not found).

create.help: Create a new folder in OneDrive. Creates a folder with the given name. By default creates at the root of the drive. Use --parent to specify a different parent folder. Conflict behavior options: - fail: Return error if folder already exists (default) - rename: Automatically rename the new folder - replace: Replace the existing folder
create.usage_instructions: Use to create a new folder. Requires DRIVE_ID and NAME as positional args. Use --parent/-p to specify parent folder path (defaults to root). Use --conflict/-c to control behavior when folder exists: fail (default), rename, or replace.

arguments.name: drive_id
arguments.help: The drive ID to create the folder in

options.name: --conflict
options.help: Conflict behavior: fail, rename, or replace

create-link.help: Create a sharing link for a folder. Creates a shareable link that grants access to the folder. Returns the Permission resource with the sharing link URL. Link types: - view: Read-only access (default) - edit: Read-write access - embed: Embeddable link (OneDrive Personal only) Link scopes: - anonymous: Anyone with the link (may be disabled by admin) - organization: Only members of your organization - users: Specific users (requires additional invite step)
create-link.usage_instructions: Use to create a shareable link for a folder. PATH is required (e.g., 'Documents/Reports'). Use --type/-t for link type: view (default), edit, or embed. Use --scope/-s for access scope: anonymous, organization, or users. Use --expiration/-e for time-limited links (ISO 8601 format). Use --drive/-d to specify drive.

options.name: --drive
options.help: Drive ID (uses ONEDRIVE_DRIVE_ID env var or default drive if not specified)

options.name: --type
options.help: Link type: view (read-only), edit (read-write), or embed

options.name: --scope
options.help: Link scope: anonymous (anyone), organization (org members), users (specific). Defaults to org setting

options.name: --password
options.help: Password for the sharing link (OneDrive Personal only)

get.help: Get folder metadata by path. Returns folder metadata as JSON if the folder exists. Exits with code 1 if the folder doesn't exist (useful for existence checks in scripts). Drive resolution order: 1. --drive option if provided 2. ONEDRIVE_DRIVE_ID environment variable if set 3. User's default OneDrive drive
get.usage_instructions: Use to get folder metadata or check if a folder exists (exit code 1 = not found). PATH is a required positional arg (e.g., 'Documents/Reports'). Drive is resolved from --drive/-d, then ONEDRIVE_DRIVE_ID env var, then default drive. Use --properties/-p to select specific fields.

options.name: --drive
options.help: Drive ID (uses ONEDRIVE_DRIVE_ID env var or default drive if not specified)

invite.help: Invite specific people to access a folder. Sends sharing invitations to the specified email addresses, granting them access to the folder. By default sends an email notification. Roles: - read: View-only access (default) - write: Edit access
invite.usage_instructions: Use to share a folder with specific people via email invitation. PATH and EMAILS (comma-separated) are required positional args. Use --role/-r for permission level: read (default) or write. Use --message/-m to include a message in the invitation email. Use --no-email to grant access without sending notification. Use --expiration/-e for time-limited access.

options.name: --drive
options.help: Drive ID (uses ONEDRIVE_DRIVE_ID env var or default drive if not specified)

options.name: --message
options.help: Optional message to include in the invitation email

options.name: --no-email
options.help: Don't send email notification to recipients

items.usage_instructions: File and folder operations — list, get, upload, download, and delete items. Most commands require DRIVE_ID as the first positional arg. Items can be referenced by ID or path (e.g., '/Documents/file.txt'). Use `items list` to browse folder contents, `items upload` for files (auto-selects simple vs resumable upload based on size).

delete.help: Delete a file or folder from OneDrive. Item can be specified by ID or by path. Use --force to skip the confirmation prompt.
delete.usage_instructions: Use to delete a file or folder. Requires DRIVE_ID and ITEM_REF (ID or path) as positional args. Use --force/-F to skip confirmation prompt. This is destructive and moves the item to the recycle bin.

arguments.name: item_ref
arguments.help: Item ID or path to delete

download.help: Download a file from OneDrive. Item can be specified by ID or by path.
download.usage_instructions: Use to download a file from OneDrive to a local path. Requires three positional args: DRIVE_ID, ITEM_REF (item ID or path like '/Documents/file.txt'), and LOCAL_PATH (where to save).

arguments.name: item_ref
arguments.help: Item ID or path to download

arguments.name: local_path
arguments.help: Local path to save the file

get.help: Get details for a specific item. Item can be specified by ID or by path.
get.usage_instructions: Use to get metadata for a specific file or folder. DRIVE_ID and ITEM_REF are required positional args. ITEM_REF can be an item ID or a path (e.g., '/Documents/file.txt'). Use --properties/-p to select specific fields like id, name, size, webUrl.

list.help: List items in a drive folder. List files and folders in the specified drive. If path is omitted, lists items at the root of the drive.
list.usage_instructions: Use to list files and folders in a drive. DRIVE_ID is required; PATH is optional (defaults to root). Use --table/-t for formatted output. Default limit is 100; adjust with --limit/-l. Use --filter/-f for field-based filtering (field:op:value). Use --properties/-p to select specific fields.

arguments.name: drive_id
arguments.help: The drive ID to list items from

options.name: --limit
options.help: Maximum number of items to return

upload.help: Upload a file to OneDrive. Automatically uses simple upload for files < 4MB, resumable upload for larger files.
upload.usage_instructions: Use to upload a local file to OneDrive. Requires three positional args: DRIVE_ID, LOCAL_PATH (local file), and REMOTE_PATH (destination, e.g., '/Documents/file.txt'). Automatically uses simple upload for files under 4MB and resumable upload for larger files.

arguments.name: drive_id
arguments.help: The drive ID to upload to

link.usage_instructions: Sharing link management — create, list, update, get, and delete sharing links and permissions on items. All commands require DRIVE_ID and ITEM_ID positional args. Use `link list` to find permission IDs needed for update/delete/get operations.

create.help: Create a sharing link for a file or folder. Creates a new sharing link that can be shared with others. The link type determines the permissions granted: - view: Read-only access - edit: Read-write access - embed: Embeddable link (for files that support embedding) The scope determines who can use the link: - anonymous: Anyone with the link (no sign-in required) - organization: Only members of your organization - users: Only specific people (use invite instead)
examples: onedrive link create DRIVE_ID ITEM_ID --scope anonymous
onedrive link create DRIVE_ID ITEM_ID --type edit --scope organization
onedrive link create DRIVE_ID ITEM_ID --scope anonymous --expiration 2025-12-31T23:59:59Z
create.usage_instructions: Use to create a shareable link for a file or folder. Requires DRIVE_ID and ITEM_ID positional args. Use --type/-t for link type: view (default), edit, or embed. Use --scope/-s for access scope: anonymous, organization, or users. Use --expiration/-e for time-limited links (ISO 8601). Use --password/-p for password-protected links (OneDrive Personal only).

arguments.name: item_id
arguments.help: The item ID to create a sharing link for

options.name: --type
options.help: Link type: view, edit, or embed

options.name: --scope
options.help: Link scope: anonymous (anyone), organization, or users

options.name: --password
options.help: Password for the link (OneDrive Personal only)

delete.help: Delete a sharing link or permission. Removes access for the specified sharing link or permission. Use 'onedrive link list' to find the permission ID.
examples: onedrive link delete DRIVE_ID ITEM_ID PERM_ID
onedrive link delete DRIVE_ID ITEM_ID PERM_ID --force
delete.usage_instructions: Use to revoke a sharing link or permission. Requires DRIVE_ID, ITEM_ID, and PERMISSION_ID positional args. Use --force/-F to skip confirmation. Use `link list` first to find the permission ID.

get.help: Get details of a specific sharing link or permission.
examples: onedrive link get DRIVE_ID ITEM_ID PERM_ID
onedrive link get DRIVE_ID ITEM_ID PERM_ID --table
get.usage_instructions: Use to get details of a specific permission or sharing link. Requires DRIVE_ID, ITEM_ID, and PERMISSION_ID positional args. Use `link list` first to find the permission ID.

list.help: List all sharing links and permissions on an item. Shows all permissions including sharing links, direct shares, and inherited permissions.
list.usage_instructions: Use to see all permissions and sharing links on an item. Requires DRIVE_ID and ITEM_ID positional args. Returns permission IDs needed for update/delete/get operations. Use --table/-t for formatted output.

arguments.name: item_id
arguments.help: The item ID to list permissions for

update.help: Update a sharing link's permissions. Update the roles or expiration of an existing sharing link or permission. Use 'onedrive link list' to find the permission ID. Roles: - read: View-only access - write: Edit access
examples: onedrive link update DRIVE_ID ITEM_ID PERM_ID --roles write
onedrive link update DRIVE_ID ITEM_ID PERM_ID --expiration 2025-12-31T23:59:59Z
onedrive link update DRIVE_ID ITEM_ID PERM_ID --roles read --expiration 2025-06-30T00:00:00Z
update.usage_instructions: Use to modify an existing sharing link's role or expiration. Requires DRIVE_ID, ITEM_ID, and PERMISSION_ID positional args. Use `link list` first to find the permission ID. Use --roles/-r to change access level: read or write. Use --expiration/-e to set new expiration (ISO 8601).

usage_instructions: Use for managing OneDrive for Business files, folders, drives, and sharing links via Microsoft Graph API. Commands follow `onedrive <group> <action>` pattern. Most commands support --table/-t for table output, --properties/-p for field selection, and --filter/-f for filtering. Requires authentication via `onedrive auth login` first. Drive ID is required for most item operations; use `onedrive drives list` to find it.
